content:
$ whoami Technology teacher turned offensive security operator. No engineering degree, a transition built through certifications, late nights on Ghidra, and a lot of broken VMs.
This blog documents what I learn. No fluff, no theory without practice.
CERTIFICATIONS Badge Certification Status OSCP - Offensive Security Certified Professional Achieved CRTP - Certified Red Team Professional Achieved CRTE - Red team Expert Achieved CETP - Certified Evasion Techniques Professional In progress ROADMAP Phase Certification Provider Status 1 CETP Altered Security In progress 2 MalDev Academy MalDev Academy Planned 3 CAPE HTB Planned 4 CRTO Zero-Point Security Planned TECHNICAL STACK (Most of them are in progress) EDR Evasion & Kernel Windows Internals, PE Structure, Kernel Callbacks, ETW Patching, PPL Bypass, Rootkit Development, Driver Exploitation
Active Directory Kerberos Abuse, ADCS, Cross-Forest Attacks, BloodHound, Rubeus, Impacket, CrackMapExec
Maldev Process Injection, Sleep Obfuscation, Stack Spoofing, Shellcode Loaders, AMSI Bypass — C/C++, Python, Assembly x64
Tools Ghidra, x64dbg, PE-bear, Volatility3, Havoc, Sliver, Chainsaw
WHAT SETS ME APART Most offensive profiles know how to attack. Few can explain what they do and why it works. Coming from teaching forced me to structure analysis and make it readable for a report, a write-up, or a team that does not live in a terminal.
CONTACT GitHub : github.com/0xCapss Email : 0xCap55@proton.me
